role_title: Associate Manager, New Verticals - Logistics Strategy & Operations

location.office_time_required: some time in home office, in person via offsites and testing products in the field

level_signals: Associate Manager title within Strategy & Operations
Base comp range $105,400–$155,000 consistent with mid-senior IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 4-6+ years of professional experience. Ideally you have experience in product operations, financial services, logistics, consulting, engineering, launch operations, or business operations.
You're analytical. You have a strong analytical background, advanced SQL proficiency, and a deep understanding of AI tooling.
You have past experience building models from scratch, you use data to forecast outcomes.
You solve problems from first principles. You're excited to solve forecasting and operational problems in innovative ways, and you resist "this is how it's always been done" thinking.

You're equally comfortable operating at 10,000 feet and 1 foot. You don't hesitate to get in the weeds with SQL and data analysis, but you're just as comfortable influencing roadmap discussions and strategic priorities.
You have strong business acumen and understand how forecast decisions impact cost, service levels, and network performance.
You have a completed Bachelor's Degree.

scope.surface: New Verticals Logistics — forecasting, demand/labor prediction, and operational efficiency for DashMarts and third-party grocery/convenience/alcohol/retail deliveries
scope.business_model: mixed first-party (DashMarts) and third-party marketplace (grocery, convenience, alcohol, retail); B2C delivery platform
core_responsibilities: Serve as DRI for forecast accuracy and continuously improving demand and labor prediction
Identify forecast variance drivers, develop hypotheses, and test model and product changes
Transform analytical insights into operational experiments creating measurable improvements in forecast precision
Write complex SQL queries, draft product requirements, and support GTM efforts
Assess operational and financial impacts of forecast improvements and model tradeoffs
Influence cross-functional team and drive alignment on roadmap priorities

green_flags: Comprehensive benefits package described in detail
Clear ownership scope: DRI for forecast accuracy
Diverse cross-functional exposure (Product, Engineering, Data Science, Ops)
Applications accepted on ongoing basis — not a hard close date
Role sits under office of COO — high visibility
red_flags: No visa sponsorship information stated — unclear for international candidates
Hybrid model described vaguely ('some time in home office, offsites, and field') — exact office cadence not specified
No specific cities listed for the role despite mentioning NYC notice (may require relocation or travel unclear)
4-6+ years range phrased as '4-6+' which is ambiguous — could mean 6+ is expected
